D7 Print L5 Evaluate Reference

CMYKOGV Extended Gamut

three extra inks — orange, green, violet — push the printable polygon outward into colours four-colour process can never reach

Four-colour process can only print inside the hexagon spanned by its primaries and secondaries (red, green, blue overprints). Adding orange, green, and violet inks replaces those muddy two-ink overprints with vivid single inks, so the gamut polygon bulges outward — capturing saturated oranges, greens, and purples that CMYK clips. Chromaticities here are illustrative.

Switch between the two ink sets: colours inside the active gamut show at full saturation, the rest grey out. Watch the oranges, greens, and violets light up when the extra inks join, and read the coverage gain.
